sn22.101:1.1: Near Sāvatthī. There the Blessed One said, “I tell you, monks:
sn22.101:1.2: It is for one who knows & sees that there is the ending of effluents.
sn22.101:1.3: For one who knows & sees what is there the ending of effluents?
sn22.101:1.4: ‘Such is form, such its origination, such its passing away.
sn22.101:1.8: Such is consciousness, such its origination, such its passing away.’
sn22.101:1.9: It is for one who knows & sees in this way that there is the ending of effluents.
sn22.101:2.1: “Even though this wish may occur to a monk who dwells without devoting himself to development—
sn22.101:2.2: ‘O that my mind might be released from effluents through lack of clinging!’—still his mind is not released from effluents through lack of clinging.
sn22.101:2.4: From lack of developing, it should be said.
sn22.101:2.6: The four establishing of mindfulness, the four right exertions, the four bases of power, the five faculties, the five strengths, the seven factors for awakening, the noble eightfold path.1
sn22.101:3.1: “Suppose a hen has eight, ten, or twelve eggs:
sn22.101:3.2: If she doesn’t cover them rightly, warm them rightly, or incubate them rightly,
sn22.101:3.3: then even though this wish may occur to her—
sn22.101:3.4: ‘O that my chicks might break through the egg shells with their spiked claws or beaks and hatch out safely!’—
sn22.101:3.5: still it is not possible that the chicks will break through the egg shells with their spiked claws or beaks and hatch out safely.
sn22.101:3.8: Because the hen has not covered them rightly, warmed them rightly, or incubated them rightly.
sn22.101:3.9: In the same way, even though this wish may occur to a monk who dwells without devoting himself to development—
sn22.101:3.10: ‘O that my mind might be released from effluents through lack of clinging!’—still his mind is not released from effluents through lack of clinging.
sn22.101:3.12: From lack of developing, it should be said.
sn22.101:3.14: The four establishing of mindfulness, the four right exertions, the four bases of power, the five faculties, the five strengths, the seven factors for awakening, the noble eightfold path.
sn22.101:4.1: “Even though this wish may not occur to a monk who dwells devoting himself to development—
sn22.101:4.2: ‘O that my mind might be released from effluents through lack of clinging!’—still his mind is released from effluents through lack of clinging.
sn22.101:4.4: From developing, it should be said.
sn22.101:4.6: The four establishing of mindfulness, the four right exertions, the four bases of power, the five faculties, the five strengths, the seven factors for awakening, the noble eightfold path.
sn22.101:5.1: “Suppose a hen has eight, ten, or twelve eggs
sn22.101:5.2: that she covers rightly, warms rightly, & incubates rightly:
sn22.101:5.3: Even though this wish may not occur to her—
sn22.101:5.4: ‘O that my chicks might break through the egg shells with their spiked claws or beaks and hatch out safely!’—
sn22.101:5.5: still it is possible that the chicks will break through the egg shells with their spiked claws or beaks and hatch out safely.
sn22.101:5.8: Because the hen has covered them, warmed them, & incubated them rightly.
sn22.101:5.9: In the same way, even though this wish may not occur to a monk who dwells devoting himself to development—
sn22.101:5.10: ‘O that my mind might be released from effluents through lack of clinging!’—still his mind is released from effluents through lack of clinging.
sn22.101:5.12: From developing, it should be said.
sn22.101:5.14: The four establishing of mindfulness, the four right exertions, the four bases of power, the five faculties, the five strengths, the seven factors for awakening, the noble eightfold path.“
sn22.101:6.1: Just as when a carpenter or carpenter’s apprentice sees the marks of his fingers or thumb on the handle of his adze
sn22.101:6.3: ‘Today my adze handle wore down this much, or yesterday it wore down that much, or the day before yesterday it wore down this much,’
sn22.101:6.4: still he knows it is worn through when it is worn through.
sn22.101:6.5: In the same way, when a monk dwells devoting himself to development, he does not know,
sn22.101:6.6: ‘Today my effluents wore down this much, or yesterday they wore down that much, or the day before yesterday they wore down this much,’ still he knows they are worn through when they are worn through.“
sn22.101:6.7: Just as when an ocean-going ship, rigged with masts & stays, after six months on the water, is left on shore for the winter: Its stays, weathered by the heat & wind, moistened by the clouds of the rainy season, easily wither & rot away.
sn22.101:6.8: In the same way, when a monk dwells devoting himself to development, his fetters easily wither & rot away.”
